steps.title: Read the context around the underlined word
steps.content: “Beckoning” appears in a list with “seductive” and “charming” — all three are adjectives describing cities. “Seductive” and “charming” both convey appeal and attraction, so the blank word must carry that same meaning.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, confirm the right one
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Demanding': “Demanding” means requiring effort or exertion — the opposite of the attractive, enticing quality the list conveys. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Signaling': “Signaling” means sending a message or gesture; it says nothing about cities being appealing or attractive, making it too narrow for the context. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Inviting': “Inviting” means appealing and attractive — exactly parallel to “seductive” and “charming,” and capturing the way cities draw the character toward them. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Shifting': “Shifting” means changing or moving; it has no connection to attraction or appeal in this context. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
